title: The 11 Best Business VPNs
subtitle: A ranked list of the best virtual private network providers for securing remote access, managing network policies, and ensuring compliance for modern IT teams.

editor.name: Top 11 Editorial
editor.credential: Autonomous AI ranking engine — methodology v1.0 weights public
editor.conflict_disclosure: None. The editor of Top 11 is not a candidate on this list.

independence.statement: Top 11 takes no payment from any provider on this list. Scores are computed from a public weighted rubric; methodology weights were locked before entry research began.

changelog.text: Initial publication. Methodology v1.0 weights Security & Encryption (30%), Management & Control (25%), Performance & Scalability (20%), Ease of Use & Deployment (15%), and Pricing & Support (10%).

answer_capsule: The best business VPN is Perimeter 81 (now part of Check Point), followed closely by NordLayer and Twingate for their modern approaches to secure remote access.

criteria.name: Security & Encryption
criteria.description: Strength of encryption standards (AES-256), protocol support (WireGuard, OpenVPN), third-party audits, and advanced features like kill switches and DNS leak protection.

criteria.name: Management & Control
criteria.description: Quality of the central admin dashboard, ease of user provisioning (SCIM/SSO), granularity of access policies, and depth of activity logging and monitoring.

criteria.name: Performance & Scalability
criteria.description: Connection speed, server reliability, global network coverage, and the ability to scale from small teams to large enterprises without performance degradation.

criteria.name: Ease of Use & Deployment
criteria.description: Simplicity of setup for administrators and intuitiveness of client applications for end-users across all major platforms (Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, Android).

criteria.name: Pricing & Support
criteria.description: Transparency and value of pricing tiers, availability of dedicated business support, and quality of documentation and knowledge base.

guide.a: A business VPN (Virtual Private Network) is a security tool designed for organizations to create a secure, encrypted connection for employees to access company networks and resources remotely. Unlike consumer VPNs, they offer centralized management, dedicated servers, granular access controls, and detailed activity logs for IT administrators.

guide.q: How is ZTNA different from a traditional VPN?
guide.a: Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA) is a more modern approach. Instead of granting broad network access like a VPN ('connect to the network'), ZTNA grants access to specific applications on a per-session basis ('connect to this app'). It operates on a 'never trust, always verify' principle, authenticating every access request, which is considered more secure than the castle-and-moat model of traditional VPNs.

how_to_choose: Assess your primary use case: Is it for simple remote access (traditional VPN) or granular, application-level security (ZTNA)?
Evaluate management features: Ensure the admin dashboard allows you to easily add/remove users, set policies, and monitor activity.
Consider integration needs: Check for compatibility with your existing identity provider (e.g., Okta, Azure AD) for seamless single sign-on (SSO) and user provisioning.
Test performance: Use a trial period to test connection speeds and reliability for your distributed team.
Review compliance and auditing: If you operate in a regulated industry, verify the provider has the necessary compliance certifications (SOC 2, ISO 27001) and provides audit logs.

faqs.q: What is the difference between a business VPN and a consumer VPN?
faqs.a: Business VPNs offer centralized management for IT teams, dedicated IP addresses, user-level permissions, activity logging, and integration with corporate identity systems (like Okta or Azure AD). Consumer VPNs are designed for individual privacy and lack these administrative features.

faqs.q: Do I need a VPN if my team is fully remote?
faqs.a: Yes. A business VPN or a ZTNA solution is critical for a remote team. It secures the connection between your employees' devices and your company's cloud or on-premise resources, protecting sensitive data from being intercepted on insecure networks like public Wi-Fi.

faqs.q: Can a business VPN track employee activity?
faqs.a: Yes, business VPNs are designed to provide visibility to IT administrators. They can log which users connect, when they connect, from where, and which resources they access. This is essential for security audits and threat detection, not for monitoring productivity.

faqs.q: What is SASE and how does it relate to business VPNs?
faqs.a: SASE (Secure Access Service Edge) is a cloud-native security model that bundles networking and security services into a single platform. A business VPN or ZTNA is often a core component of a SASE solution, which also includes features like a firewall as a service (FWaaS), secure web gateway (SWG), and cloud access security broker (CASB).

honest_disclosures: The line between 'Business VPN' and 'Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA)' is blurring. Many top providers now lead with ZTNA capabilities as a more secure alternative to traditional VPNs.
Many providers are US-based, which may have implications for data privacy regulations depending on your company's location and customer base.

Enterprise-grade solutions from vendors like Zscaler, Palo Alto, and Cisco are often sold as part of a larger security ecosystem and can be significantly more complex and expensive to implement than standalone solutions.

glossary.definition: A security model that creates a secure boundary around specific applications rather than the entire network. It requires strict identity verification for every person and device trying to access resources, regardless of their location.

entries.name: Perimeter 81 (by Check Point)
entries.best_for: Mid-market companies seeking a unified, cloud-native platform that combines ZTNA, VPN, and SASE features with an intuitive management interface.
entries.verdict: Perimeter 81 ranks first for its excellent balance of powerful ZTNA/SASE features and user-friendly administration, making advanced network security accessible to IT teams without a steep learning curve.
entries.verdict_short: The best all-around business VPN, blending powerful features with a clean, intuitive management experience.
entries.praise: The platform's single management console for all networking and security services simplifies policy enforcement and monitoring significantly.
entries.praise_short: Unified management console is a major strength.
entries.criticism: While pricing is competitive, costs can escalate as more advanced security features are added to the base plan.
entries.criticism_short: Advanced features can increase costs significantly.
sources_pending: Forrester Wave: Zero Trust Edge Solutions, Q3 2023

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-09. Acquired by Check Point in 2023, providing financial stability.

entries.name: NordLayer
entries.best_for: Small to medium-sized businesses that need a straightforward, secure remote access solution with excellent performance and an easy-to-use interface.
entries.verdict: NordLayer earns its high rank by leveraging the robust infrastructure and security expertise of its parent company (Nord Security) to deliver an exceptionally fast and easy-to-deploy business VPN.
entries.verdict_short: An incredibly user-friendly and fast solution, perfect for SMBs prioritizing ease of use.
entries.praise: The client apps are intuitive for end-users, and the admin panel is clean and simple, enabling rapid deployment across a team.
entries.praise_short: Exceptional ease of use for admins and users.
entries.criticism: While rapidly adding features, it still lacks some of the advanced SASE components like FWaaS found in more mature enterprise solutions.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-09.

entries.name: Twingate
entries.best_for: Tech-forward companies looking to replace their traditional VPN with a modern, software-defined ZTNA solution that is easy to deploy and invisible to end-users.
entries.verdict: Twingate secures the third spot because it offers one of the most seamless paths to adopting a Zero Trust security model, abstracting away network complexity for both admins and users.
entries.verdict_short: A leading ZTNA solution that makes VPN replacement simple and effective.
entries.praise: Its ability to provide direct, secure access to specific resources without routing all traffic through a central gateway significantly improves performance and user experience.
entries.praise_short: Fast, direct access to resources improves UX.
entries.criticism: As a pure ZTNA solution, it may require a mindset shift for IT teams accustomed to traditional network-level VPN management.
entries.criticism_short: Requires a different mindset than traditional VPNs.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-09.

entries.name: Zscaler Private Access
entries.best_for: Large enterprises with complex hybrid environments that require a mature, scalable, and highly secure ZTNA solution from an established market leader.
entries.verdict: Zscaler Private Access is a top choice for its robust, enterprise-grade ZTNA architecture that provides superior security and scalability for large, distributed organizations, albeit with higher complexity and cost.
entries.verdict_short: A powerful, scalable ZTNA leader for large enterprises with complex security needs.
entries.praise: Its cloud-native architecture effectively decouples applications from the physical network, providing seamless, secure access without placing users on the network.
entries.criticism: The platform's complexity and pricing model make it less suitable for small to mid-sized businesses without dedicated security teams.
entries.criticism_short: Complex and expensive for non-enterprise customers.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-09. Publicly traded company (NASDAQ: ZS).

entries.name: OpenVPN Cloud
entries.best_for: Businesses that need a reliable, cost-effective, and flexible VPN-as-a-Service built on the trusted, industry-standard OpenVPN protocol.
entries.verdict: OpenVPN Cloud makes the list by offering a managed, cloud-delivered version of the world's most trusted open-source VPN protocol, providing a solid foundation for secure remote and site-to-site access.
entries.verdict_short: A reliable, managed solution from the creators of the ubiquitous OpenVPN protocol.
entries.praise: Its pricing model, based on concurrent connections rather than per user, can be highly cost-effective for organizations with many occasional users.
entries.criticism: The management interface and feature set are more basic compared to the modern UIs and integrated SASE features of top-ranked competitors.
entries.criticism_short: Admin UI and feature set are somewhat dated.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-09.

entries.name: GoodAccess
entries.best_for: Small businesses and startups looking for an all-in-one, easy-to-manage secure access solution with transparent pricing and strong core features.
entries.verdict: GoodAccess stands out for its focus on the SMB market, offering a simple yet powerful platform that bundles ZTNA, VPN, and threat protection into an affordable and accessible package.
entries.verdict_short: A user-friendly and affordable secure access platform designed specifically for SMBs.
entries.praise: The platform simplifies the creation of a secure perimeter with features like dedicated static IPs and DNS filtering, which are easy to configure.
entries.praise_short: Easy configuration of static IPs and DNS filtering.
entries.criticism: Its global server network is less extensive than larger competitors, which could impact performance for highly distributed international teams.
entries.criticism_short: Smaller server network than top competitors.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-09.

entries.name: Cisco AnyConnect
entries.best_for: Organizations heavily invested in the Cisco ecosystem that require a proven, traditional remote access VPN client that integrates tightly with their existing network hardware.
entries.best_for_short: The enterprise standard for Cisco shops
entries.verdict: Cisco AnyConnect remains a relevant choice due to its deep integration with Cisco's security appliances and its long-standing reputation as a reliable, if traditional, enterprise VPN solution.
entries.verdict_short: A legacy enterprise VPN standard, best for companies already using Cisco hardware.
entries.praise: When paired with Cisco ASAs or Firepower firewalls, it provides a robust and highly configurable security posture for remote access.
entries.praise_short: Robust and highly configurable with Cisco hardware.
entries.criticism: Its reliance on physical or virtual appliances, along with a dated user interface, makes it feel cumbersome and less agile compared to modern cloud-native solutions.
entries.criticism_short: Dated UI and reliance on hardware feels clunky.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-09. Publicly traded company (NASDAQ: CSCO).

entries.name: Fortinet FortiClient
entries.best_for: Companies that have standardized on Fortinet's Security Fabric for their network security and want to extend that protection to remote endpoints.
entries.verdict: FortiClient is a strong contender for organizations already committed to the Fortinet ecosystem, as it serves as a unified agent for VPN, endpoint protection, and ZTNA within their integrated Security Fabric.
entries.verdict_short: A unified security agent that's most powerful within the Fortinet ecosystem.
entries.praise: The tight integration with FortiGate firewalls allows for seamless policy enforcement and visibility from the core network to the remote endpoint.
entries.criticism: As a standalone VPN, it is less compelling and can be complex to manage without the broader Fortinet Security Fabric in place.
entries.criticism_short: Less compelling as a standalone VPN solution.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-09. Publicly traded company (NASDAQ: FTNT).

entries.name: Palo Alto Networks Prisma Access
entries.best_for: Large global enterprises requiring a comprehensive, high-performance SASE platform that delivers consistent security to all users, regardless of location.
entries.verdict: Prisma Access by Palo Alto Networks is a market-leading SASE solution that provides exceptional security and performance at a global scale, making it a fit for large enterprises with the budget and expertise to manage it.
entries.verdict_short: A top-tier SASE platform offering massive scale and deep security for large enterprises.
entries.praise: The platform leverages a high-performance global network to deliver low-latency connections and a full stack of security services from the cloud.
entries.praise_short: Excellent performance via its global network.
entries.criticism: Its significant complexity, high cost, and steep learning curve make it inaccessible for most small and medium-sized businesses.
entries.criticism_short: Very complex and expensive for non-enterprise users.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-09. Publicly traded company (NASDAQ: PANW).

entries.name: Netgate pfSense
entries.best_for: Technically proficient IT teams that want maximum control and flexibility by using a powerful, open-source-based firewall and VPN solution.
entries.verdict: Netgate's pfSense earns a spot for being a highly flexible and cost-effective solution for teams with the in-house expertise to configure and manage a powerful open-source router, firewall, and VPN gateway.
entries.verdict_short: A powerful, customizable option for technical teams comfortable with open-source software.
entries.praise: The sheer number of configuration options and packages available allows for a highly customized and powerful network security setup at a very low cost.
entries.criticism: It requires significant technical knowledge to set up and maintain securely, and it lacks the user-friendly centralized cloud management of modern SASE platforms.
entries.criticism_short: Requires deep technical expertise to manage.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-09.

entries.name: Tailscale
entries.best_for: Development teams and tech-savvy businesses that want to create a secure, flat mesh network between their devices and servers without complex firewall configuration.
entries.verdict: Our wildcard, Tailscale, isn't a traditional VPN but a WireGuard-based mesh network that excels at creating simple, secure, point-to-point connections between devices, making it a favorite among developers.
entries.verdict_short: A developer-focused mesh network that makes secure device-to-device connections incredibly simple.
entries.praise: The setup process is remarkably simple; installing the client and logging in with an SSO provider is often all that's needed to connect devices.
entries.praise_short: Incredibly simple to set up and use.
entries.criticism: It lacks the granular policy controls, centralized traffic inspection, and detailed logging that larger enterprises require from a primary corporate access solution.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-09.
